story.title: Stringham_Tammy_OH2767 COVID-19 Oral History

lines.line: # Introductions
> Interviewer (Stephanie Milne-Lane and interviewee (Tammy Stringham) introduce themselves. The interviewer provides the structure of the interview.
**Lehmhi County Economic Development Association, Covid-19, pandemic**
keywords: **Lehmhi County Economic Development Association, Covid-19, pandemic**

metadata.title: Introductions
metadata.synopsis: Interviewer (Stephanie Milne-Lane and interviewee (Tammy Stringham) introduce themselves. The interviewer provides the structure of the interview.
metadata.keywords: Lehmhi County Economic Development Association, Covid-19, pandemic

lines.line: #Interviewee Background

>Tammy Stringham provides contextual background on her life. She describes how and when she moved to Idaho. She also described how she became the Executive Director of the Lemhi Economic Development Association.
*economic development, Mackay, Salmon, Blackfoot, Arco**

metadata.title: Interviewee Background
metadata.synopsis: Tammy Stringham provides contextual background on her life. She describes how and when she moved to Idaho. She also described how she became the Executive Director of the Lemhi Economic Development Association.

lines.line: #COVID-19

>Tammy Stringham shares how she first learned about COVID-19 and describes what it was like living in Salmon when Governor Brad Little ordered all people to stay at home to slow the spread of COVID-19. She also details how she she managed her staff.
*pandemic, Innovation Center, Lemhi Ride, work from home, masking**

lines.line: #Lemhi County Economic Development Association
>Tammy Stringham supplies background and organizational structure of the Lemhi County Economic Development Association. She also describes how the COVID-19 pandemic impacted Lemhi County economy.

**5013C, Lemhi Ride, Salmon Valley Business and Innovation Center, Airport Industrial Park, Idaho Travel Council, Idaho Department of Commerce, USDA Rural Development Grant, COVID-19, local food systems**

lines.line: #Economic Development

>Tammy Stringham provided a insight into the hardships Lemhi County confronted during the pandemic. Topics covered include housing and workforce. She also touches on remote work and businesses having to adapt to new challenges. Tammy also offers insight into leasing office space for incubating new businesses. Discussion about how Lemhi County businesses rebounded from the shut down and the use of government grants/relief is also touched on.
**Baby Boomers, COVID-19, economic, development, housing market, workforce, telecommuting, Small Business Association, PPP Loan, EIDLE loan, Mofi **
keywords: **Baby Boomers, COVID-19, economic, development, housing market, workforce, telecommuting, Small Business Association, PPP Loan, EIDLE loan, Mofi **

lines.line: #Supply Chain
>Tammy Stringham touches upon the problems the COVID-19 pandemic had on the supply chain. She provides examples of issues she dealt with in her retail fly fishing shop.
**fly fishing, fishing rods, supply chain**

lines.line: #Role as Executive Director

>Tammy Stringham describes how she assisted small businesses during the pandemic. She touches on how she learned new information and advocated for Lemhi County businesses. She also talks about her support system. She also discusses what type of businesses took advantage of grants and financial assistance during the pandemic.
**Small Business Association, Small Business Development Centers, Zoom, workshops, networking, COVID-19, Challis, hair salons**
keywords: **Small Business Association, Small Business Development Centers, Zoom, workshops, networking, COVID-19, Challis, hair salons**

story.description: Discussion with Lemhi County Economic Development Association Executive Director Tammy Stringham about COVID-19 and the business community. Topics discussed included health precautions, government aid, technology, and business adaptation.
